title: A short walk I love
summary: Describe a short walk you take often — where it is and what you see along the way.
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a short walk you take often. Say where it is and what you see. Use present simple.
hintStarters: On the way, I can see…
At the end of the walk, I…

hintVocab.meaningVi: hình phản chiếu trên mặt nước

sampleText: I often walk along the canal near my home in Amsterdam. The walk is about twenty minutes. I always take the same path because it is very quiet and beautiful. On the left side, there are tall trees. On the right side, there is the water of the canal. I can see many houseboats on the water. Some of them have flowers on the roof. I also see ducks. They swim slowly near the edge of the canal. There are old cobblestone bridges along the way. I usually stop on one bridge and look at the reflection of the houses in the water. It is very pretty. Many people ride bicycles on the path, but they go slowly here. At the end of the walk, there is a small bench under a big tree. I sometimes sit there and drink from my water bottle. Then I walk home. This walk makes me feel calm and happy.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đi theo cùng một con đường
hình phản chiếu của những ngôi nhà

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for regular actions
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn để nói về những việc bạn làm thường xuyên: 'I often walk', 'I always take the same path', 'I sometimes sit there'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for things you do regularly: 'I often walk', 'I always take the same path', 'I sometimes sit there'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: There is / There are for describing places
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'There is' (với danh từ số ít) và 'There are' (với danh từ số nhiều) để mô tả những gì có ở một nơi: 'There is a bench', 'There are tall trees'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'There is' with singular nouns and 'There are' with plural nouns to describe what exists in a place: 'There is a small bench', 'There are tall trees'.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi thường đi bộ dọc theo kênh đào gần nhà ở Amsterdam. Đoạn đường đi bộ khoảng hai mươi phút. Tôi luôn đi theo cùng một con đường vì nó rất yên tĩnh và đẹp. Phía bên trái có những cây cao. Phía bên phải là mặt nước của kênh đào. Tôi có thể thấy nhiều thuyền nhà trên mặt nước. Một số có hoa trên mái. Tôi cũng thấy những con vịt. Chúng bơi chậm rãi gần mép kênh. Dọc đường có những cây cầu lát đá cuội cũ kỹ. Tôi thường dừng lại trên một cây cầu và nhìn hình phản chiếu của những ngôi nhà trên mặt nước. Rất đẹp. Nhiều người đi xe đạp trên con đường, nhưng họ đi chậm ở đây. Cuối đoạn đường có một chiếc ghế dài nhỏ dưới một cây to. Đôi khi tôi ngồi đó và uống nước từ bình của mình. Sau đó tôi đi bộ về nhà. Chuyến đi bộ này khiến tôi cảm thấy bình yên và hạnh phúc.

mcQuestions.prompt: Where does the writer take this walk?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I often walk along the canal near my home in Amsterdam'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the type of waterway mentioned.

mcQuestions.prompt: How long does the walk take?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The walk is about twenty minutes'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the time given for the walk.

mcQuestions.prompt: What is on the left side of the path?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'On the left side, there are tall trees'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for what is on the left side.

options: There are a duck on the water.
There are many ducks on the water.
There is many ducks on the water.
There are duck on the water.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'There are' is used with plural nouns: 'many ducks' is plural.
mcQuestions.hint: Check whether the noun is singular or plural.

options: Near the edge of the canal
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'They swim slowly near the edge of the canal'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find where the ducks are in the water.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the writer see when standing on the bridge?
options: The reflection of the houses in the water
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I look at the reflection of the houses in the water'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for what the writer sees from the bridge.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses an adverb of frequency correctly?
options: I take always the same path.
Always I take the same path.
I always take the same path.
I take the same path always before.
mcQuestions.explanation: Adverbs of frequency go before the main verb: 'I always take'.
mcQuestions.hint: Place the adverb between the subject and the main verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Where is the bench at the end of the walk?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'there is a small bench under a big tree'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the preposition that tells you where the bench is.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes what someone sees on a walk?
options: I cook dinner and watch television.
I see ducks and houseboats along the canal.
I write emails and read reports.
I do my homework at the desk.
mcQuestions.explanation: This sentence describes things you see outdoors along a canal.
mcQuestions.hint: Pick the sentence that describes outdoor sights.

mcQuestions.prompt: How does the walk make the writer feel?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'This walk makes me feel calm and happy'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look at the last sentence of the text.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do regular short walks in nature or along water help people feel better? What do you think happens in the mind during a quiet walk?
